user_request: Draft a suit for recovery of Rs.20,00,000/- paid as advance for a business transaction which failed due to Defendant's default. Plead total failure of consideration under Section 65 of Indian Contract Act. Claim refund with interest and costs. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court.

facts.summary: Plaintiff seeks recovery of Rs. 20,00,000 paid as advance for a business transaction that failed due to the Defendant's default. The suit alleges total failure of consideration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act and claims a refund with interest and costs.

missing_fields: Date of failure of transaction/cause of action
assumptions: Jurisdiction lies with the District Court based on valuation or cause of action
The advance was paid for a specific agreement which subsequently failed

164.353 Where the defendant had been served with summons, he must apply to set aside an ex parte decree 
passed against him within 30 days of the decree under Art. 164, and not within 30 days of his knowledge of the

imitation. Under art 166 of Sch I of the Limitation Act, 1908, an application under r 90 had to be made within

the fraud of the decree-holder or other parties to the sale been kept concealed from the judgment-debtor, he is 
entitled to apply under r 90, whether the sale has been confirmed or not, and the time for making the application is 
to be computed from the date when the fraud first became known to him. 36(s 17 of the Limitation Act, 1963). In a

IN THE COURT OF THE District Court (Commercial Division) AT {{COURT_PLACE}}

Commercial Suit No. __________ / 20____

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, Aged about {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

PLAINTIFF: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged about {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}.
DEFENDANT: {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged about {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.


JURISDICTION

JURISDICTION: This Hon'ble Court has territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction to try and entertain the instant suit. The Plaintiff paid Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh) as an advance to the Defendant for a business transaction described herein, within the local limits of this Court. The cause of action arose at {{TRANSACTION_PLACE}} on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} in relation to the said transaction. The Defendant resides at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}} within the jurisdiction of this Court. The value of the suit is within the pecuniary limits of this Court.


FACTS OF THE CASE

1. The Plaintiff is a {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}} residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}} and carries on {{PLAINTIFF_BUSINESS_DESCRIPTION}}.
2. The Defendant is a {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}} residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}} and is known to the Plaintiff in connection with the business transaction described as {{TRANSACTION_DESCRIPTION}}.
3. On {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}}, the Plaintiff paid Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only) as an advance to the Defendant towards the proposed business transaction described as {{TRANSACTION_DESCRIPTION}} dated {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}}. The payment was made by {{PAYMENT_MODE}} and a proof of payment is annexed as Annexure B.
4. The parties agreed that the Defendant would perform the obligations by {{OBLIGATION_DATE}} and the Plaintiff would rely on the Defendant's performance for the completion of the transaction.
5. The Plaintiff duly paid the advance amount and did not receive the goods, services, or other consideration promised by the Defendant, nor any return of the advance.
6. On {{DEFAULT_DATE}}, the Defendant wholly defaulted by failing to perform the transaction and by failing to refund the advance, thereby causing total failure of consideration.
7. The Plaintiff made repeated oral and written demands for refund on dates {{DEMAND_DATES}}; copies of reminders are annexed as Annexure C.
8. On {{NOTICE_DATE}}, the Plaintff served a legal notice by registered post A.D. demanding refund of Rs. 20,00,000 along with interest; a true copy of the notice and postal receipts are annexed as Annexure D.
9. The Defendant did not comply with the demands within the statutory period, and the available reply, if any, was evasive and unsatisfactory.
10. As the Defendant retains the sum without performing any consideration, the Plaintiff has suffered loss by depriving him of the use of funds and consequently seeks relief from this Hon'ble Court.


LEGAL BASIS

11. Primary: Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 provides that where there is total failure of consideration, the party who has received money or value under an agreement is bound to restore it. In the present case, Rs. 20,00,000/- was paid by the Plaintiff to the Defendant for a business transaction which has failed due to the Defendant's default, resulting in total failure of consideration.
12. Without prejudice and in the alternative, the Plaintiff pleads Sections 70 and 72 of the Indian Contract Act as additional grounds for restitution of money received under a contract that has become void or voidable, and for money paid in advance where the promised consideration was not furnished. The facts show that the Defendant received Rs. 20,00,000/- as advance for a transaction that did not materialize; restitution is warranted to prevent unjust enrichment.
13. Defensive Pleading (to pre-empt likely defences): The Plaintiff anticipates possible defences such as (a) no part performance by the Defendant; (b) there being a set-off or counter-claim; and (c) denial of total failure of consideration. The Plaintiff submits that the relief sought is warranted notwithstanding any such potential defences and prays for appropriate relief.


CAUSE OF ACTION

14. The cause of action first arose on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} when the Plaintiff paid Rs. 20,00,000/- as advance for the proposed business transaction.
15. The cause of action further arose on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} when the Defendant defaulted by failing to perform or offer to perform the contract and did not refund the amount or provide any adequate substitute consideration.
16. The cause of action is a continuing one as the Defendant continues to retain the advance without furnishing any consideration, thereby depriving the Plaintiff of the use of his funds, and the right to recover such money continues to accrue day by day within the jurisdiction of this Court.


LIMITATION

Article 22 of the Limitation Act, 1963
Description: For money received by the defendant for the plaintiff's use; Period: Three years.
Accrual: When the money is received by the defendant for the plaintiff's use. In the present case, the accrual date is {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}}, and the suit is filed within three years from that date, satisfying the period of limitation.


VALUATION AND COURT FEE

17. The suit is valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only) for purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. The requisite court fee at ad valorem rate is payable on the suit valuation as per the applicable Court Fees Act. The exact court fee amount is indicated as {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} in the accompanying documents.


INTEREST

18. Interest:
(a) Pre-suit interest: From {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} to the date of filing of this suit, at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum, amounting to approximately Rs. {{PRE_SUIT_INTEREST_AMOUNT}}; (b) Pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC, at a rate deemed just and reasonable during the pendency of the suit; (c) Future interest from the date of decree till realisation at {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum.


PRAYER

19. In the premises stated hereinabove, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:
(a) Decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for the refund of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only) with interest thereon from the date of payment till realisation; 
(b) Award pre-suit interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} till the date of filing of this suit;
(c) Award pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC during the pendency of the suit at such rate as this Hon'ble Court may deem just and reasonable;
(d) Award future interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from the date of decree till actual realisation;
(e) Award costs of this suit including court fees and any pre-litigation costs incurred for issuing the legal notice;
(f) Grant such other and further relief as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.


LIST OF DOCUMENTS

ANNEXURES:
20. Annexure A — Primary transaction document / agreement relating to the business transaction
21. Annexure B — Proof of payment / performance by Plaintiff (payment proof)
22. Annexure C — Copies of reminders / communications demanding refund
23. Annexure D — Legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} with postal receipt and AD card
24. Annexure E — Defendant's reply to the legal notice (if any) or proof of non-reply


VERIFICATION

VERIFICATION
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby solemnly verify and state on oath that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 23 of this plaint are true and correct to my personal knowledge based upon the records and documents available with me, that the contents relating to legal submissions are based upon legal advice received and are believed to be correct, that no part of the cause of action has arisen within the jurisdiction of any other court, and that nothing material has been concealed therefrom. Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate

{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}

In the present case, Rs. 20,00,000/- was paid by the Plaintiff to the Defendant for a business transaction which has failed due to the Defendant's default, resulting in total failure of consideration.
12. Without prejudice and in the alternative, the Plaintiff pleads Sections 70 and 72 of the Indian Contract Act as additional grounds for restitution of money received under a contract that has become void or voidable, and for money paid in advance where the promised consideration was not furnished. The facts show that the Defendant received Rs. 20,00,000/- as advance for a transaction that did not materialize; restitution is warranted to prevent unjust enrichment.
13. Defensive Pleading (to pre-empt likely defences): The Plaintiff anticipates possible defences such as (a) no part performance by the Defendant; (b) there being a set-off or counter-claim; and (c) denial of total failure of consideration. The Plaintiff submits that the relief sought is warranted notwithstanding any such potential defences and prays for appropriate relief.


CAUSE OF ACTION

14. The cause of action first arose on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} when the Plaintiff paid Rs. 20,00,000/- as advance for the proposed business transaction.
15. The cause of action further arose on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} when the Defendant defaulted by failing to perform or offer to perform the contract and did not refund the amount or provide any adequate substitute consideration.
16. The cause of action is a continuing one as the Defendant continues to retain the advance without furnishing any consideration, thereby depriving the Plaintiff of the use of his funds, and the right to recover such money continues to accrue day by day within the jurisdiction of this Court.


LIMITATION

Article 22 of the Limitation Act, 1963
Description: For money received by the defendant for the plaintiff's use; Period: Three years.
Accrual: When the money is received by the defendant for the plaintiff's use. In the present case, the accrual date is {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}}, and the suit is filed within three years from that date, satisfying the period of limitation.


VALUATION AND COURT FEE

17. The suit is valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only) for purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. The requisite court fee at ad valorem rate is payable on the suit valuation as per the applicable Court Fees Act. The exact court fee amount is indicated as {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} in the accompanying documents.


INTEREST

18. Interest:
(a) Pre-suit interest: From {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} to the date of filing of this suit, at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum, amounting to approximately Rs. {{PRE_SUIT_INTEREST_AMOUNT}}; (b) Pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC, at a rate deemed just and reasonable during the pendency of the suit; (c) Future interest from the date of decree till realisation at {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum.


PRAYER

19. In the premises stated hereinabove, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:
(a) Decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for the refund of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only) with interest thereon from the date of payment till realisation; 
(b) Award pre-suit interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} till the date of filing of this suit;
(c) Award pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC during the pendency of the suit at such rate as this Hon'ble Court may deem just and reasonable;
(d) Award future interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from the date of decree till actual realisation;
(e) Award costs of this suit including court fees and any pre-litigation costs incurred for issuing the legal notice;
(f) Grant such other and further relief as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.
